name: Kabardian
notes: Grapheme inventory NOT YET SPECIFIED (stub). Kabardian (East Circassian) is written in a Cyrillic alphabet. It is a stub not because the script is exotic but because the orthography is overwhelmingly DIGRAPHIC and TRIGRAPHIC: a ~48-consonant inventory (ejectives, labialised and pharyngealised series) is mapped onto the Cyrillic base with multi-character sequences — кӏ, кӏу, къ, къу, кхъ, кхъу, гъ, гъу, лъ, лӏ, пӏ, тӏ, фӏ, хь, хъ, ху, цӏ, щӏ, ӏу — including the palochka ⟨Ӏ⟩, so a correct longest-match grapheme table has to be built and verified before any map can be claimed. Latin and Arabic-script orthographies exist historically (diaspora) and are not modelled here. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode kaba1278), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.
